description: DSSE attestation verification vectors for SemVer-Trust (spec §4.3, §8.1-§8.2; docs/conformance-crypto-fixtures.md §6). Envelopes are vendored frozen bytes (signed bytes cannot be patched, only regenerated) built by build-attestation-envelopes.py; payloads use the real v0.1 and v0.2 predicate-type URIs with fake fixture-local subjects and test-only keys. Signature convention: the DSSE pre-authentication encoding (PAE) is signed as an OpenSSH SSHSIG with namespace attestation@semver-trust.dev; sig is the base64 of the armored SSHSIG; keyid is the signer's SHA256 fingerprint. The verifier consumes attestations/allowed_signers as injected trust material at the pinned verification_time (ADR-018), validates the payload against the registered JSON Schemas, and MUST distinguish a schema-invalid payload from an invalid signature — both abort, with distinct errors.

vectors.description: A review attestation (§4.3) signed by the merge workflow's machine identity: signature verifies, payload validates against review-v0.1.json.
A release attestation (§8.1) with a full, internally coherent provenance vector over the signed-history fixture commits: signature verifies, payload validates against release-v0.1.json.
A signed review/v0.2 successor attestation verifies and validates against the v0.2 schema, including qualified-review and profile-identity fields.
A signed release/v0.2 successor attestation verifies and validates against the v0.2 schema, including interval, policy, version-state, threshold, and profile-identity bindings.

The signature is genuine but the payload omits the required 'decision' block: schema-invalid. The error MUST be distinct from a signature failure — a well-signed lie about shape is a different defect from a forgery (§8.2, GO-033 acceptance).
The payload was altered after signing (timestamp changed; still schema-valid): the signature no longer covers the bytes. Distinct from schema_invalid.

A well-formed envelope signed by a key absent from the attestation registry: the storage layer is never the trust anchor, the signature inside the attestation is (§8.2) — and an unenrolled signature anchors nothing.
